---
title: 'Captain Theron &quot;Ash&quot; Ashland, USAF Spec Ops.'
date: '2015-12-02T05:02:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '5248397907704874873'
blogger_filename: '/2015/12/captain-theron-ashland-usaf-spec-ops.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Character Sketch'
---

<h3>Captain (retired) Theron&nbsp;"Ash" Ashland, USAF Spec Ops</h3><br /><br /><strong>Age</strong>:  Ash was born on 07/31/1968, which makes him 47 years old as of 12/04/15. He was medically discharged from the USAF 8 years ago (2007), after serving his entire adult life (18-39) 21 years. <br /><br /><strong>Description</strong>: Over 6' tall, brown hair (graying), blue eyes.<br /> <br /> <strong>Tattoo</strong>: The Air Force Special Tactics motto is&nbsp;tattooed on his left forearm. <span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot;;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">"<a href="https://www.airforce.com/careers/detail/special-tactics-officer" target="_blank">First There</a>...That Others May Live."</span></span><br /><span style="font-family: Georgia;"></span><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot;;"><strong>Scar</strong>: runs up the left side of his neck, he got it???</span><br /><br /><h4>Getting into the background: Ash</h4><br />His mother passed away giving birth to his younger sister. <br /><br />While Ash was in college in Oregon, his sister Jennie (one year younger) went to Biola University in southern California. When she was at an event in down town LA, performing, she was killed accidentally in a drive shooting&nbsp;by El Maduro, a Latino gang from Los Angeles. They were attempting to a revival gang member present on the scene.<br /><br />After his sister died, his father (a pastor) killed himself too. Leaving Ash with the house, and no family. Ash was already in his senior year getting a B.S. in Military Science, and he went into the USAF after that year (to run away from the memories and pain).<br /><br />His lack of family ties made him an excellent candidate for the unsanctioned Operation White Noise, which was the military wing of the experiment.<br /><br />Ash was a volunteer in White Noise while he was a USAF Captain,&nbsp;Special Tactics Officer. <br /><br />As a result of the experiment, he lost all memories from about 3 weeks prior to the experiment to 3 weeks afterward. This included the time period in which he volunteered. Therefore, he has no memory of it. He only knows that he woke with 12 weeks of missing memory (6 of, 3 before and after), with no explanation. He also knows that he's had strange PTSD side effects. He was medically discharged with little to no explanation. He was told that he was on a mission and recovered from behind enemy lines, and that's it. Which was a lie on behalf of his leaders. <br /><br />He was medically discharged 8 years ago. <br /><br />He moved into his parents home (which he's owned since his father's passing), a two story cabin style home in the woods of Ashland Oregon.<br /><br />The losses of his youth,&nbsp;later his memories, and his eventual PTSD symptoms left Ash a broken man. <br /><br />Internally, he jumps at the slightest hint of danger and&nbsp;he sees things that aren't there. But despite that, he strives for a normal life. His medications and&nbsp;counseling appointments keep him fairly stable. <br /><br />He sees clouds on nearly everyone, every day. On some people he sees actual creatures.&nbsp;Some sightings are clearer than others. Sometimes he sees a colorful cloud grow into a creature, then disappear into cloud again. Mist, smoke, vapor? Hm... <br /><br />After 8 years, he is generally able to shield his reactions from physically showing to all but the most keen observer. <br /><br />As part of his therapy, he writes what he sees in a notebook that he keeps in his pocket. He doesn't use cell phones or technology. <br /><br />Due to his own paranoia, he does keep unregistered weapons, a stash of burner phones, cash, passports, and transportation all at three&nbsp;safe houses. One local, two remote. He also knows the location of empty facilities in the immediate area that he can make use of at any given moment.<br /><br />As part of his ritual to keep life normal, he works in his workshop making furniture and carvings from the wood on his property (25 acres). He brings the items into town every few days to sell through a local craftsman consignment store, Woodmen Furnishings.<br /><br />He also stops by the Grounded Cafe' every Monday and Wednesday morning to sit and observe the locals. He has a Veterans support group every Wednesday evening. On Sundays he heads into town for Church on Sunday morning, and to the local Pub on Sunday afternoons (during football season).<br /><br />This particular Wednesday morning, he's just got his coffee when he's interrupted by crazy Eddie outside. He knows him, he usually acts up every few months, but never this bad. Then he sees creatures on Eddie, talking to him. He's attacked, spilling his coffee onto the ground. That's never happened before. He tries not to hurt him, and the police come and take&nbsp;Eddie away.<br /><br />Later, on his way&nbsp;to the support group, he meets a woman stranded on the side of the road. She gets into his car for a ride into town. While driving to town, they are rammed off the road off a cliff, but survive. He sees a light creature (not an angel, make is something else, animal, rabbit? wolf? something), just before going off the cliff.<br /><br />He comes to, injured, the girl is fine, but trapped. There is a man at the top of the cliff holding a gun. Instinct tells him that this is no accident, and he runs with the girl into the forest. They are tracked by the hit man. This is when she confides that she is a reporter, that Dr. Briar was interested in Ash, and she thinks they are up to something.<br /><br />Ash leads evades the hit man, brings the girl to his safe house, and he gets his weapons. The safe house is shot up, and Ash kills the hit man. They then take off in his back up vehicle (or motorcycle) and head out of town. <br /><br /><strong>Action!</strong><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot;;"></span><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot;;"></span><br /><h4>********************&nbsp; Old Bio below, trying something new above. **************************</h4><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot;;"></span><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span>Chester was born on 01/01/1986, which makes him 29 years old as of 12/04/15.<br /><div><br /></div>Ash is 47 in the year 2015. His Date of Birth: 07/31/1968<br /><br />Eta is 35 in 2015. Her date of birth, 12/06/1980.<br /><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"></span><span class="Apple-tab-span" style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif; white-space: pre;"> </span><br /><div><div style="margin: 0in 0in 0in 0.5in; text-indent: -0.25in;"><div style="margin: 0in 0in 0in 0.5in; text-indent: -0.25in;"><div style="margin: 0px;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span style="font-family: &quot;symbol&quot;;">Â·<span style="font-family: &quot;times new roman&quot;; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</span></span><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;">Character Sketch Template (<a href="https://www.udemy.com/blog/character-sketch-examples/">Hints</a>)(<a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Now,_Discover_Your_Strengths" target="_blank">Strengths List</a>)(<a href="https://www.isa.org/templates/two-column.aspx?pageid=134103&amp;append=35734" target="_blank">Strengths details</a>)(<a href="http://www.16personalities.com/personality-types" target="_blank">16P-Types</a>)(<a href="http://www.16personalities.com/articles/our-theory" target="_blank">16P-Theory</a>)<o:p></o:p></span></span></div></div></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;">Hello my name is Amy Truthfinder, and Iâ€™m here with&nbsp;</span>Colonel Theron "Ash" Ashland, USAF Spec Ops.&nbsp;<span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;">from the White Noise series, to find out&nbsp;more about him/her and get a little behind the scenes information.</span></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><b><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">In the way of introductions:<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">Ash is a Primary character in the book White Noise. He has chosen to side with the Good Guys.</span><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">Ash was born on 07/31/1968, which makes him 47 years old as of 12/04/15. He was medically discharged from the USAF 8 years ago (2007), after serving his entire adult life (18-39) 21 years. He draws a full pension, plus hazard disability, and a check from his post as Dean of Military Science at <a href="https://www.sou.edu/mil-sci/index.html" target="_blank">SOU</a>.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">As I'm sitting down with him now, I can tell you that he is&nbsp;<span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;">6'3", well built, brown eyes, brown hair worn in a slightly longer than military cut. There is a small scar on his neck, under his left ear.&nbsp;</span></span><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;">He is wearing blue jeans, a t-shirt, and a black flack jacket. The A</span>ir Force Special Tactics motto is embroidered across the back, which matches the tattoo on his left forearm.</span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">"<a href="http://www.airforce.com/special-operations/#/badge-combat-controller" target="_blank">First There</a>...That Others May Live."</span></blockquote></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><b><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">Personality and Motivations:<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">Tell me, what is your 16P Type, what are you top five Strengths finders results, and what one-three words best describes you?<o:p></o:p></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">I'm an ISTP-T, with strengths in Strategic, Achiever, Responsibility, Learner, and Focus&nbsp;</span></blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;">I am&nbsp;</span><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;">exhausted by social interaction. My friends describe as&nbsp;</span><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;">pragmatic and down-to-earth.</span>&nbsp;I&nbsp;<span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;">tend to hide my feelings, even from myself. I see efficiency as more important than cooperation. I specialize at&nbsp;</span><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;">improvising and spotting opportunities. I'm flexible, relaxed, and a nonconformist. I don't want to be locked down to a single plan, I want my options open</span>. I'm also a p<span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;">erfectionist and eager to improve.&nbsp;</span></span></blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">One word, huh? Hmm.. Defender! It's tattooed on my arm after all.</span></blockquote><ul style="background-color: white; color: #252525; font-family: sans-serif; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22.4px; list-style-image: url(data:image/svg+xml,%3C%3Fxml%20version%3D%221.0%22%20encoding%3D%22UTF-8%22%3F%3E%0A%3Csvg%20xmlns%3D%22http%3A%2F%2Fwww.w3.org%2F2000%2Fsvg%22%20version%3D%221.1%22%20width%3D%225%22%20height%3D%2213%22%3E%0A%3Ccircle%20cx%3D%222.5%22%20cy%3D%229.5%22%20r%3D%222.5%22%20fill%3D%22%2300528c%22%2F%3E%0A%3C%2Fsvg%3E%0A); margin: 0.3em 0px 0px 1.6em; padding: 0px;"><li style="margin-bottom: 0.1em;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif; font-size: small;">Strategic - one who is able to see a clear direction through the complexity of a situation</span></li><li style="margin-bottom: 0.1em;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif; font-size: small;">Achiever - one with a constant drive for accomplishing tasks</span></li><li style="margin-bottom: 0.1em;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif; font-size: small;">Responsibility - one who, inexplicably, must follow through on commitments</span></li><li style="margin-bottom: 0.1em;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif; font-size: small;">Learner - one who must constantly be challenged and learning new things to feel successful</span></li><li style="margin-bottom: 0.1em;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif; font-size: small;">Focus - one who requires a clear sense of direction to be successful</span></li></ul><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;">What do you do with your&nbsp;</span><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;">time when you are not working?</span></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">I'm not sure what you mean? I'm on salary as the Dean of Military Science. You think there is ever a time when I'm not working? But I understand your question. There are occasions when I break away, and when I do... I read constantly. I practice playing chess against the computer. I spend time working out my body and mind. I</span></blockquote><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">What are the things you enjoy most in life?<o:p></o:p></span><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></span><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">A good history book, a java chip frapachino, and leather chair by the fireplace.</span></blockquote></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">What things just bug you to no end?<o:p></o:p></span><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></span><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">I would have to say people... who fail to follow through on their promises.</span></blockquote><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">If every job, task, and activity in the world paid the same (and it was more than enough to have everything you wanted) what would you do?</span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">Wow... that is a great question. At some level I would have to say what I'm doing now. I already receive a full pension so I do not need to work. And training the younger generation, not just students but leaders and teachers, is a special kind of reward.&nbsp;</span></blockquote><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">If you could sit down and chat with a person who is alive today, who would it be? What about anyone not alive today? What about a non-real person? What about someone from your story world?<o:p></o:p></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">A person alive today? Hm... I've met most of the military minds of our time during the service. A few presidents even. I suppose I would have to say that I suppose it would be nice to have lunch with Chuck Norris. I'd like to thank him for entertaining years on television, and his frequent work with the N.R.A.</span></blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">A non-real person? You mean like, a comic book or movie hero? I would say... You know what? Since we're imagining, I'd like to joint Captain America on a mission.&nbsp;</span></blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">A person not alive today... Abraham Lincoln. He had the fortitude to stand where few would stand. He never got to see his dream come to pass, even know it's not fully realized. But he would be proud of the progress we've made as a nation toward unity.&nbsp;</span></blockquote><div><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><b><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">Training and background<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><div style="margin: 0in 0in 0pt;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">Tell me about your schooling. What high school did you go to? Where did you go to college? What activities did you participate in? Sororities / Fraternities? Were you&nbsp; in any other groups? Did you work through school, loans, scholarships, rich family?<o:p></o:p></span><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;">I was an <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ashland_High_School_(Oregon)" target="_blank">Ashland High School </a>&nbsp;-&nbsp;Grizzly, as my father, and his father before him. I joined the ROTC on a full ride scholarship at&nbsp;</span><a href="https://www.sou.edu/mil-sci/index.html" style="font-family: georgia, serif;" target="_blank">SOU</a>, only a few blocks from my high school.&nbsp;Graduated with honors at 17.5 and went straight into boot camp on my 18th birthday. I was far too engrossed in the ROTC to be a part of any other activities. And I served in the United States Air Force until my force medical retirement in 2007.&nbsp;</span></blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">I was recruited into the Air Force Special Operations Forces (<a href="http://www.airforce.com/special-operations/" target="_blank">SOF</a>) under Combat Control, where I eventually achieved the rank of Colonel. I worked there for most of my career, until I was recruited for the U.S. Air Force Intelligence, Surveillance, and Reconnaissance (USAF <a href="http://www.dni.gov/index.php/intelligence-community/members-of-the-ic" target="_blank">ISR</a>). Of course, that was before the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Air_Force_Intelligence,_Surveillance_and_Reconnaissance_Agency#Wings" target="_blank">ISR </a>was reclassified as the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Twenty-Fifth_Air_Force" target="_blank">25th</a>.&nbsp;</span></blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">Toward the end of my career I worked closely with the National Counterterrorism Center (<a href="http://www.nctc.gov/" target="_blank">NCTC</a>), I always thought it was funny that they did not separate the words Counter and Terrorism. I worked on special assignments on joint task forces with the Marines, NSA, CIA, and others.</span></blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">It was a wild ride man. But the truth is... nothing could have prepared me for what came after retirement. Or for that matter, what caused the retirement in the first place... as you read about in White Noise, I'm sure.</span></blockquote><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;">Tell me about your major. How many degrees do you have? In what? Why that and not something else?</span></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">I majored in Political Science, with a minor in Military Science. I only ever wanted to be a soldier, from as early as I can remember. Until recently, I was enjoying training them.&nbsp;</span></blockquote><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">Unit recently? How so?</span><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">Well, that's what this book is all about. Let's just say my life was disrupted when men broke into my home to kill me. And I've been on a wild ride ever since.&nbsp;</span></blockquote><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">What was your first job? How many jobs have you had? What was your favorite job? Least favorite job? What do you do now?<o:p></o:p></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">My first job was sweeping and cleaning up at the local family owned hardware store, Smith's Hardware.&nbsp;</span></blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">Jim Smith was a great guy, taught me a lot about discipline, and being creative with tools. I used to really enjoy people coming in and asking him: "Jim. I want to do this or that..." and man you could see the wheels in his head turn. You know, I once saw him turn a handful of scrape wood into a working Spinning Wheels for a local Lama Farmer?&nbsp;</span></blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">Since my father died when I was young, Jim became a surrogate father to me. He was the best man I ever knew. He took me in when my mom and sister both died. He helped me get through my last two years of high school. He put me to work more as a way to channel my energy, than because he needed a worker.</span></blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">The only life I've ever know after that was the <a href="http://www.af.mil/" target="_blank">USAF</a>, and now I'm back in my home town at my home college. Excuse me... University now, we upgraded.</span></blockquote><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">Do you hold any licenses, specialties, etc.?<o:p></o:p></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">I hold licenses for Concealed Carry and the American Sniper Association (ASA). I also cleared for Top Secret (<a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Security_clearance#Hierarchy_2" target="_blank">TS SCI</a>) while I was working under the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Security_clearance#Hierarchy_2" target="_blank">USAF-ISR</a>. I had other special classifications who's titles themselves are classified, not currently available to the public.</span></blockquote><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">If you could switch jobs today what would you do? (Real and Imagined)</span><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">If you'd asked me a while back, before this whole new adventure started. I would have said that I was happy as Dean of Military Science at SOU. Now? I think I'll follow this trail and see where it leads. I don't know... maybe I'll stay at SOU, and pursue this on the side? We'll see.</span></blockquote><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></span><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">OK... let's transition back for a moment, and take a look at your life before all of this.</span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0in 0in 0pt;"><b><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">Family history<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><div style="margin: 0in 0in 0pt;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">What are your parents name? Do you know your grandparents? Do you have an siblings? Cousins, Aunts, Uncles, Other important relatives? Are you related to anyone famous?<o:p></o:p></span><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">Unfortunately, that's a sad story. I have none living.&nbsp;</span></blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;">My Father was Captain Theodore Ashland. He died in 1974, when I was just 6 years old. He died in the Vietnam War, like so many other good men.&nbsp;</span><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;">I remember him of course, but not much. He was gone so often with the war. It's almost like the memory of a memory. Most of what I know about him is from pictures, journals, and stories from extended family. Most of those family are gone now too though.</span></span></blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">I had one sister, her name was Mary. She died when she was 12, I was 15 at the time. It was a car accident. August 1985. The roads weren't paved well then. The car was being driven by a 13 year old coming in from the farm. That wasn't uncommon in farm communities back then. There's no telling if an adult would have handled it differently anyway, roads were bad then too.&nbsp;</span></blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">They would &nbsp;would just tar the road, throw gravel down, and let the cars run the gravel into the tar. It created for less traction than we have today.&nbsp;</span></blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">The tires slipped on the gravel, and he slid right into her. Wrapped the car around the street lamp. Pinned her, on her bike...&nbsp;</span></blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">I'm sorry...&nbsp;</span></blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">Pinned her on her bike between the car's side and the street lamp. The kid driver was thrown through the windshield. The passenger, 15, went to the ER, but he lived. It was such a tragedy. For both families. I still get a card from Eddie, the survivor, every year. In August.&nbsp;</span></blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">My mom's name was Gwen Ashland (McGee was her maiden name). She died in 1985 from cancer, I was just 16 at the time. After that, with no living relatives left, I moved in with Jim Smith.</span></blockquote></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">I'm sorry to hear that. You're life... it was a very hard life growing up. I know this is a sensitive topic for you. Let me give you &nbsp;a moment. How about we switch to a lighter topic.</span><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">What does your name mean, and why did you parents choose it?<o:p></o:p></span><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></span><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><a href="http://www.thenamemeaning.com/theron/" target="_blank">Theron </a>is originally a Greek name. It means hunter. In English it's means Untamed. It's played well into my life hunting down terrorist cells around the world, and... removing them from the field.&nbsp;</span></blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">Ashland is my family name. As far as we know my family came to the USA in the early 1800's and settled around TN. My great grandparents wanted to move out to the west, and they'd heard how green and beautiful the state of Oregon was.</span></blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">They caught wind that a town had been established by the name of Ashland, and they thought it was a "Sign" from God. So they moved. We had been settled in the town every since. That is... until now. I guess I'm the last Ashland from my line still in Ashland OR.</span></blockquote><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">Is there anything interesting or important or unusual we need to know about your family?<o:p></o:p></span><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></span><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">Besides my parents death's when I was young? Not that I know of at this time.</span></blockquote><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">What was your relationship like with your Mother? Father? Others?<o:p></o:p></span><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></span><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">I barely knew my father, but I loved him when I was young. My mom worked two jobs to keep my sister and I taken care of. It's probably the stress that eventually did her in. But she was warm, and funny. She loved to laugh. She rarely had a harsh word to say. I loved her very much.&nbsp;</span></blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">My sister and I were inseparable until she died. That's probably why I latched onto Eta as my partner at the ISR, she reminded my of my sister. Fiesty, full of spirit. She would get her way, or you'd hear it.&nbsp;</span></blockquote></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><b><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">Early Childhood through middle school<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><div style="margin: 0in 0in 0pt;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">What experiences, growing up, marked your memory? In other words, what can you remember about your childhood, what stands out?<o:p></o:p></span><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></span><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;">I don't remember much. I was in boy scouts, school sports, the usual stuff through middle school. Growing up without a dad was hard, but I managed. Most of the good times were erased with the passing of my mother and sister within a year of each other. So... I just try not to think about the past.</span></span></blockquote></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">Who were your best friends?<o:p></o:p></span><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></span><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;">I would not say that I ever had best friends. I had friends, plenty. People that came to the house, played games, etc. I managed to find myself in various groups growing up. But friends? That just feels too strong a word for them... they were...&nbsp;acquaintances.&nbsp;</span></span></blockquote><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">Worst enemies, bullies?<o:p></o:p></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">No bullies in my life. There were a few who tried to bully my kid sister now and then, but when they learned I was there, they left her alone. I always saw myself as the protector of the other kids from bullies. I was naturally fast and strong. I rarely had an ounce of fat on me, even as a baby. That made it hard to get bullied.&nbsp;</span></blockquote><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">What was your best day? Worst?<o:p></o:p></span><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></span><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;">Goes without mentioning. My worst days were the loss of my family.</span></span>&nbsp;</blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></span><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , serif;">My best day... I'll have to let you know when I have it.</span></span></blockquote></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><h4><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span>Alternate info</h4></div><div style="margin: 0in 0in 0pt;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;">   </span>1. Innocuous, blend in to the crowd, disappear, but look a little cool while doing it.</span><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;">   </span>2. His ways are set, until he is forced into hiding, when he must make changes to his look.</span><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;">   </span>1. Marksman, best shooter with hand gun or rifle in the USAF, several years in a row, before taking missions that would not allow him to compete. Now that he's retired, he still shoots on the ranges.</span><br /><div><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;">   </span>1. Avid Reader</span></div><div><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;">   </span>2. Despite his hard exterior, he much prefers to think through an issue, than bulldoze into it. He's reading allows him to stretch his mind so that he can appear to be acting fast, when he's really taking in every aspect of a situation, and making an informed decision.</span></div><div><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">Alternate Version of sisters' death</span></div><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;">   </span>1. The Accident of 1984: When Ash was 16, he was driving the farm truck with a girlfriend, and his sister was in the back of the cab. He was driving too fast through the small town streets, and the gravel of the new road was loose. He hit the curve too hard, slide sideways and wrapped the truck around a power pole. The girlfriend (Holly Beady - 15), and his sister (Jeannie Ashland - 12) were killed instantly. Ash was taken to a nearby hospital unconscious, but with no life threatening injuries.</span><br /><div><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;">   </span>2. Formative: &nbsp;He felt that it was that day that broke his Mom's heart, which led to her death a year later, and made him swear that he was only good for destruction, and that but that he would use that destruction to protect and serve.</span></div><div><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;">  </span>E. Conflicts</span></div><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;">   </span>1. Inner Conflicts:</span><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;">    </span>1) I'm only good for destruction.</span><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;">    </span>2) I want to serve, protect, and prevent.</span><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;">   </span>2. Outer Conflicts</span><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;">    </span>1) Victim of a Mysterious Experiment.</span><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;">    </span>2) Wants to avoid people</span><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><span class="Apple-tab-span" style="white-space: pre;">    </span>3) Gets sucked into a situation where he's called to serve and protect.</span><br /><div style="margin: 0in 0in 0pt;"></div><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><br /><div style="margin: 0in 0in 0pt;"><div style="margin: 0px;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><br /><div style="margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0in;"><b><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">Here are some photos that remind me of you:<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><div style="margin: 0in 0in 0pt;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><br /><div style="margin: 0in 0in 0pt;"></div><br /><div style="margin: 0in 0in 0pt;"><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/patches-combatcontrol-27616f737250.png" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" height="225" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/patches-combatcontrol-c7cd2d0c7d38.png" width="400" /></a></div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/image010-739950-06c904d1645b.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" height="189" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/image010-739950-113aabd80276.jpg" width="320" /></a></div><br /><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/air-drop-2-183e0b57be5e.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" height="208" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/air-drop-2-8cd10ed9914a.jpg" width="320" /></a></div><br /><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/combat-controllers-a6506f24c51e.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" height="213" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/combat-controllers-a32d188083ea.jpg" width="320" /></a></div><br /><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/it-27s-not-science-fiction-a4ba8e9cb164.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" height="320" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/it-27s-not-science-fiction-fbbf09cda7ab.jpg" width="215" /></a></div><br /><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/usaf-38ae7d76c955.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/usaf-38ae7d76c955.jpg" /></a></div><br /><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/leather-jacket-29d6bda28ffc.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" height="320" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/leather-jacket-822c4e5aeb3b.jpg" width="238" /></a></div><br /><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/air-drop-79892d593f44.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/air-drop-79892d593f44.jpg" /></a></div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><br /></div><div style="margin: 0px;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0px;"><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/combat-control-tatoo-3c038d5a78f3.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" height="282" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/combat-control-tatoo-94d48c460fc5.jpg" width="320" /></a></div><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0px;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0px;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span></div></div></div></div></div><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><br /><h4 style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: Verdana, Geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 15.4px; line-height: 19.6px; margin: 0px; position: relative;"><div style="font-weight: normal;"><b><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif; font-size: small;">Comment on this post, or Hit me up on Twitter&nbsp;<a href="https://twitter.com/darrellwolfe" style="color: #149adf; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">@DarrellWolfe&nbsp;</a>,&nbsp;Google&nbsp;<a class="g-profile" data-gapiattached="true" data-gapiscan="true" data-onload="true" href="https://plus.google.com/106337514196493406365" style="color: #149adf; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">+DarrellWolfe</a>, Facebook&nbsp;<a href="https://www.facebook.com/groups/907869989282584/" style="color: #149adf; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">DarrellGWolfe</a>,&nbsp;</span></b></div></h4><blockquote class="tr_bq" style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: Verdana, Geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 15.4px; line-height: 19.6px;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif; font-size: small;"><br /></span></blockquote><h4 style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: Verdana, Geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 15.4px; line-height: 19.6px; margin: 0px; position: relative;"><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif; font-size: small;">By&nbsp;<a href="https://plus.google.com/+DarrellWolfe/posts" style="color: #149adf; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">Darrell Wolfe</a></span></h4><blockquote class="tr_bq" style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: Verdana, Geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 15.4px; line-height: 19.6px;"><i><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif; font-size: small;">Storyteller, Creative,&nbsp;<a href="http://www.16personalities.com/infj-personality" style="color: #149adf; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">INFJ</a>,&nbsp;<a href="https://www.gallupstrengthscenter.com/Purchase/en-US/Product?Path=Clifton%20StrengthsFinder" style="color: #149adf; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">Intellection</a>, Ideation, Input, Learner, Achiever</span></i></blockquote><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><i><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">* I may include "Affiliate Links" which means I earn a commission when you buy some of the products I link to.&nbsp;</span></i><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><br /><ul><li><i><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">See my full about page and disclosures:&nbsp;<a href="http://authorfun.blogspot.com/p/what-is-this.html" target="_blank">Click Here</a>.&nbsp;</span></i></li></ul><ul><li><i><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">Also check out my&nbsp;<a href="http://authorfun.blogspot.com/p/affiliate-store.html" target="_blank">Resources and Suggestions&nbsp;</a>for writers (<a href="http://authorfun.blogspot.com/p/affiliate-store.html" target="_blank">here</a>).&nbsp;</span></i></li></ul><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><i></i><br /></span><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><a href="http://authorfun.blogspot.com/" target="_blank">It's Author Fun</a>&nbsp;By&nbsp;<a href="https://plus.google.com/+DarrellWolfe/posts" target="_blank">Darrell Wolfe</a></span><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">To Subscribe to the&nbsp;<a href="http://feeds.feedburner.com/AuthorFun" target="_blank">RSS: Click Here</a></span><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><br /></span><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;">For Non-Fiction stories with a lesson by&nbsp;<a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/" target="_blank">Darrell Wolfe: Click Here</a></span><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><div style="clear: both; float: left; padding-bottom: 10px; text-align: right;"><br /></div>
